steps.title: Anchor on the country named by the 2020 figure
steps.content: The student singles out the country that mined 0.55 million metric tons of copper in 2020. In the table, exactly one country has 0.55 in the 2020 column, so the rest of the sentence must report that same country's 1995 production.

steps.title: Read that country's 1995 row
steps.content: For the country whose 2020 value is 0.55, the 1995 column shows 0.26 million metric tons. The completion must use that paired value, not a 1995 number belonging to any other row.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — '0.59 million metric tons of copper in 1995.': 0.59 is a 1995 value tied to a different country's row, not the 0.55-in-2020 country. Mismatched row. ✗
  • Choice B — '0.44 million metric tons of copper in 1995.': 0.44 also belongs to a different country's 1995 entry, not the targeted country. Mismatched row. ✗
  • Choice C — '0.26 million metric tons of copper in 1995.': 0.26 is the 1995 value in the same row as the 0.55-in-2020 country, completing the comparison correctly. ✓
  • Choice D — '2.49 million metric tons of copper in 1995.': 2.49 is from a much larger producer's row and is not the 1995 figure for the targeted country. Mismatched row. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
